comment: Massachusetts: c.183C high-cost (ineligible) and 209 CMR 53 borrower's-interest (net tangible benefit) worksheet on refinances within 60 months.

rules.description: Massachusetts high-cost home mortgage loan (c.183C): high-cost loans are not originated (APR-spread proxy trigger per reference-indices)

Massachusetts borrower's-interest rule (183 §28C / 209 CMR 53): any refinance of a home loan made within the prior 60 months requires a dated Borrower's Interest Worksheet demonstrating net tangible benefit, unless a safe harbor applies (new APR at least 2.50 points below trigger vs prior first-lien loan, or FHA/VA/HFA)

artifact_always.text_template: Complete and date the Massachusetts Borrower's Interest Worksheet (209 CMR 53) demonstrating the refinance is in the borrower's interest, or document an applicable safe harbor.
